key_themes: Adjusted EPS beat with raised shareholder returns (13% dividend hike, $2B buyback expansion)
Strong digital sales growth (16-21%) and loyalty gains offsetting pharmacy/IRA headwinds
Revenue miss, opioid settlement charge (GAAP net loss), margin pressure, and competitive grocery challenges
sources_checked: Albertsons Companies IR / Q4 2025 Earnings Release
summary: Albertsons delivered an adjusted Q4 EPS beat of $0.48 (vs ~$0.43 expected), 7.7% revenue growth, higher adjusted EBITDA, strong digital acceleration, and raised its dividend 13% while expanding buybacks to $2B, with slightly improved fiscal 2026 guidance in key areas.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/albertsons-beats-q4-earnings-despite-142600754.html)[[2]](https://www.albertsonscompanies.com/investors/financial-reports/quarterly-results/default.aspx)[[3]](https://public.com/stocks/aci/earnings) The stock reacted negatively to the revenue miss, large opioid settlement (~$774M) driving a GAAP net loss, modest margin compression, and pharmacy headwinds.[[4]](https://www.investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-albertsons-beats-eps-but-misses-revenue-in-q4-2025-93CH-4613113)[[5]](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/Albertsons+Companies+Inc.+(ACI)+Releases+Q4+2025+Earnings:+Revenue+Up+but+Net+Loss+and+EPS+Drop) Biggest risk is sustained top-line pressure and margin compression from competition and digital/pharmacy mix shifts in a challenging grocery environment; analysts remain mostly constructive (Hold/Overweight consensus, ~$21-22 average PT implying material upside from ~$16.40 levels) but trimmed some targets post-earnings.[[6]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/ACI/forecast/)[[7]](https://stockanalysis.com/stocks/aci/ratings/)

recent_posts.highlight: HOLD rating post-earnings; notes resilience, digital/ loyalty catalysts and productivity program but highlights pharmacy pressures and industry trends
Stock slid after quarterly sales miss, slower GLP-1 impacts, higher gas prices, and disclosure of $774M opioid settlement
Beats on Q4 adjusted EPS despite IRA pharmacy headwinds; digital sales +16%, loyalty members +12%, adjusted EBITDA growth
EPS and EBITDA beats but revenue miss highlighting top-line growth challenges amid competitive pressures; notes dividend/buyback confidence
